frameworks.name: anatomical-physiological accounting of the visual pathway
frameworks.one_line: combine known anatomy and physiology to count neurons supporting a behavioral task.

frameworks.name: information content along the visual pathway
frameworks.one_line: neuron counts at each stage feed downstream estimates of representational efficiency.

core_question: How many retinal ganglion, LGN, and V1 neurons in the macaque are engaged by an orientation-discrimination task?
key_assumptions: macaque anatomical and physiological measurements transfer across the cited literature
neuron counts at each pathway stage can be combined multiplicatively from published densities and coverage
manipulations: computation evaluated for a specific orientation-discrimination experiment
comparison across pathway stages (RGC vs LGN vs V1)
key_findings: presents a step-by-step calculation yielding neuron-count estimates at RGC, LGN, and V1 for a discrimination task
interpretations: such per-stage counts let researchers compare information content between visual periphery and cortex
the calculation supports assessing the efficiency of computations along the pathway
connecting_signals: anatomy and physiology of primate vision
